# Does Prior Coding Experience Affect Difficulty in COMP110?

## Analysis Summary

I analyzed survey data from 534 COMP110 students to explore whether prior coding experience affects how difficult they find the course. I compared prior coding experience with difficulty ratings (rated 1-7).

## Key Findings

- 328 out of 534 students had no prior coding experience
- Students with no experience rated difficulty at an average of 4.6 out of 7
- Students with over 2 years of experience rated difficulty at only 2.3 out of 7

## Visualizations

<img src="/comp110-analysis/static/imgs/box_chart.png" alt="Box chart of difficulty by prior experience" width="600"/>

## Conclusion

The data clearly supports the idea that students with no coding background find COMP110 significantly harder. I recommend that the course could offer an optional intro module for students with no prior experience. A potential downside is the resources required to build and maintain it, but the impact on the largest group of struggling students makes it worthwhile.
